result:
The experiment is designed, pre-registered in `runs/pending_experiment.json`, and the figure passes figcheck. The harness can now take over.

**What I designed this iteration:** a lighting-canonicalizing reconstruction auxiliary — building explicitly on the library's L1 entry (flavor B, the "budget-free" version). During training only, a small throwaway decoder (~55k params) must redraw the clean daytime reference crop — same location, same heading, pixel-aligned pairs the frozen sim provides for free — from the encoder's 8×8×48 feature grid, via an L1 loss at 64×64 weighted 0.3. The decoder is deleted before export, so the flying ONNX graph is byte-identical in shape and all deployment gates are untouched.

**Why this, given the history:** the best score is 839.12 m (exp 20, per-epoch location resampling, kept), and the error profile is now flat across lighting buckets and areas — flat but mediocre, with the exp-18 probe's ~3× held-out-location gap still the binding constraint. The three attacks on that gap through place-supervision mechanisms (exps 18, 19, 22) were all reverted; their common failure was adding more memorization-shaped targets. This design goes the other way: the auxiliary task is location-generic (night→day redrawing is the same inversion everywhere on the map), so its dense gradients are the first the trunk receives that transfer to never-trained terrain by construction. Since all six lighting renders of a place share one daytime answer, the cheapest fit is features that cancel lighting and sensor noise — collapsing descriptor variance so the head's capacity concentrates on place discrimination alone. I also distinguished it from refuted exp 9 (contrastive descriptor objective, satisfiable by collapsing information, on the old from-scratch encoder) in the hypothesis.

**Prediction registered:** worst-case median improves to ~700–790 m (−6% to −16%), broadly across buckets with slightly larger gains in evening/night; refuted at ≥ 839 m.

The implementation brief spells out both file changes (a `ReconDecoder` + optional `return_feat` flag in `model/model.py`; reference-crop targets and the added L1 term in `model/train.py`), pins the rng stream so sampled locations stay identical to the current best, and restates the frozen CLI and ONNX contracts. The architecture figure reuses the kept design's inference path verbatim, with red reserved for the new training-lane decoder chain.

tool_input.command: sqlite3 /workspace/low-light-geolocalization-autoresearch/experiments.sqlite "SELECT id, title, hypothesis, conclusion FROM experiments WHERE id IN (17,18,19,20,22);" -separator $'\n---\n'
tool_input.description: Read hypotheses and conclusions of recent experiments

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT length(arch_svg) FROM experiments WHERE id IN (20,22);" && sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=22;" > /tmp/exp22.svg && head -c 3000 /tmp/exp22.svg
tool_input.description: Export exp 22 architecture SVG for reuse

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=22;" > /tmp/exp22.svg; wc -c /tmp/exp22.svg
tool_input.description: Export exp 22 architecture SVG to temp file

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=22;" > /tmp/exp22.svg
tool_input.description: Export exp 22 architecture SVG to temp file
